behaviors.description: Request to external API includes the full taxonomy label list every time.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (request builder -> API client), not just pure local logic. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network needed; a fake client can capture payload. LLM-DEP: does not depend on model judgment. Rejected System/Agentic because behavior text is about request contents, not live provider behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to an in-process fake API client, call classify(), and assert captured outbound payload contains exactly the configured taxonomy labels (complete, no omissions) across representative inputs.

behaviors.description: Out-of-taxonomy API label is mapped to "unknown".
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is normalization/validation logic on returned label; single-function behavior. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP: none (feed fixed raw label). Rejected Integration because behavior text is specifically about label mapping rule, which is fully testable at local function boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization/validation function with taxonomy and an invalid label (e.g., "medical"); assert output is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: Normalization is deterministic for identical raw API responses.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is pure determinism/idempotence of normalization step. No module handoff required, no infra, no model dependence. Rejected higher levels because behavior text explicitly scopes to normalization step output for identical input.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke normalization repeatedly with the same raw response fixture and taxonomy; assert identical final label each run.

behaviors.description: Repeated live API calls for same input stay consistent unless provider response changes.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real model/provider output stability across repeated calls; stubs hide this. REAL-INFRA also exists (real API), but Agentic is higher and required because decision output itself is under test. Rejected Workflow because this is repeated single-step classification calls, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run N real classification calls against the provider for the same document+taxonomy, record returned labels/raw provider outputs, and assert label stability conditioned on unchanged provider response.

behaviors.description: Configured token budget is enforced before dispatch (truncate/reject policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is cross-module wiring: budget check must gate dispatcher invocation and apply policy. Can be caught with in-process tokenizer/policy + fake client; no real OS/network needed. LLM output quality is irrelevant. Rejected System/Agentic because behavior text is pre-dispatch enforcement, not live provider/model behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Compose classifier with real budget enforcement component and fake API client; use over-budget input and assert policy outcome (truncate or reject) occurs before any outbound API call.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for typical document stays within configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: requires real external API call and provider-reported usage metadata; mocks can hide provider-side counting differences. LLM-DEP on semantic quality is low here; assertion is budget/usage telemetry, not classification correctness. Rejected Agentic because behavior text targets usage accounting, not model decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Send representative document to real provider through production request path, read provider token-usage fields, and assert reported total is <= configured budget.

behaviors.description: On provider rate-limit signal, module waits and retries instead of surfacing immediate error.
behaviors.justification: Failure is orchestration across retry policy, timing/backoff, and client call sequence. In-process fake provider + controllable clock can reproduce it; real network not required. No dependence on model output content. Rejected System/Agentic because behavior text is transport/error-handling control flow around the call.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake provider that returns rate-limit error then success; run classifier with fake clock/sleeper and assert wait/backoff happened and final result returned after retry rather than immediate error.
